tests.name: test_cwnd_grows_after_slow_start (test_congestion_control.TestCases)
tests.output:
Congestion control test - this test checks if the implementation returns to slow start on timeout.
Congestion window did not increase by approximately MSS after exiting slow start. Make sure to increase the congestion window by approximately MSS every round trip time after exiting slow start.
Duration of test_congestion_control.TestCases.test_cwnd_grows_after_slow_start: 3.818s

tests.name: test_fast_recovery (test_congestion_control.TestCases)
tests.output:
Congestion control test - this test checks if the implementation enters fast recovery on 3 duplicate ACKs.
Initial slow start phase...
Last window size before duplicate ACKs: 3992 bytes
Expected cwnd after fast recovery: 6127.0 bytes
Sending 3 duplicate ACKs to trigger fast recovery...
Measuring window after fast recovery...
Window size after fast recovery: 5508 bytes
Fast recovery successful! Window adjusted from 3992 to 5508 bytes (expected ~6127.0 bytes)
Duration of test_congestion_control.TestCases.test_fast_recovery: 6.017s

tests.name: test_goes_to_slow_start_on_timeout (test_congestion_control.TestCases)
tests.output:
Congestion control test - this test checks if the implementation returns to slow start on timeout.
Forcing timeout...
After timeout:
Congestion window decreased significantly after timeout.
Duration of test_congestion_control.TestCases.test_goes_to_slow_start_on_timeout: 5.818s

tests.name: test_slow_start (test_congestion_control.TestCases)
tests.output:
Congestion control test - this test checks if the congestion window increases exponentially during slow start.
  0: 2754 bytes - 2.00x
  1: 3992 bytes - 1.45x
Congestion window did not increase exponentially during slow start or started with the wrong size. Make sure to set the initial congestion window to WINDOW_INITIAL_WINDOW_SIZE.
Duration of test_congestion_control.TestCases.test_slow_start: 5.212s

tests.name: Test that the receiver ACKs the highest contiguous sequence number
tests.output:
Gap filling test - this test sends packets out of order to the receiver, then sends the missing packet and checks that the receiver ACKs the highest contiguous sequence number.
Received correct ACK 101 for first packet.
Received ACK 101 after out-of-order packet (pkt1 skipped).
Passed gap filling test. Receiver correctly ACKed 301 (highest contiguous sequence number) after the missing packet arrived.
Duration of test_retransmit.TestCases.test_gap_filling_ack: 5.011s
